rationale: Both notes indicate highly parallel work: each is a large meta-analysis of elasticity estimates using hierarchical/random-effects models and moderator coding (e.g., time trends, life-cycle, endogeneity/specification effects). Given this strong methodological and empirical-generalization overlap, the 2005 price-elasticity paper could plausibly serve as a benchmark or methodological source for the 2011 advertising-elasticity study, not just a passing mention. With only a DOI match and no in-text snippet, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
